title: selago pinguicula
intro: Selago pinguicula, also known as the "Hottentot's beard" or "pickhandle plant", is a succulent plant native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions, as well as reducing fever and promoting digestion. Selago pinguicula is often prepared as an infusion, poultice, or ointment, and has been used in traditional herbal preparations to treat a range of ailments.
